title: SACRAMENTO – Following Governor Newsom’s request for emergency legislative action to fight COVID-19, Assemblymember Ash Kalra (D-San Jose) today announced Assembly Bill (AB) 3216,

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's economic recovery hinges on the economic security of its workers.",
    "Implementing long-term policies will help get people through a crisis like COVID-19 in the future.",
    "Employees should not lose their livelihood if they miss work due to quarantine or isolation during a public health emergency like COVID-19.",
    "The current job protections are vastly inadequate in supporting workers during emergencies.",
    "AB 3216 will allow workers to use leave to care for themselves or a family member affected by COVID-19.",
    "AB 3216 will provide an additional 12 weeks of job-protected leave under the California Family Rights Act (CFRA) for COVID-19-related care.",
    "AB 3216 will ensure 'return to work' rights for employees in specific sectors during a state of emergency.",
    "AB 3216 will provide an additional 10 days of paid sick leave to employees during a state of emergency.",
    "AB 3216 will expand the uses of existing paid sick leave to cover situations related to public health orders.",
    "As of May 12, there are at least 71,046 cases of COVID-19 in California, reflecting rapid virus spread.",
    "COVID-19 is caused by a novel coronavirus that has not previously been seen in humans.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has disproportionately affected women, particularly women of color, who make up a significant portion of low-wage workers."
  ]
}
```
